descriptionShort: Winnipeg Jets trades Pete Peters and Keith Acton to Philadelphia Flyers; Moe Mantha rejoins team via waivers.
descriptionLong: The Winnipeg Jets traded Pete Peters and Keith Acton to the Philadelphia Flyers on October 3, 1989, in a deal involving draft picks and coinciding with earlier waiver activity. Moe Mantha was claimed off waivers by the Jets, returning to the team after five years away. Goaltender Daniel Berthiaume was established as the Jets' No. 1 goalie following roster moves before the season opener against the New York Rangers.

attribution.headline: Peters, Acton sent down the 'Yellow Brick Road'
